heading: Under Secretary for Memorial Affairs
label: 307 Under Secretary for Memorial Affairs
text: There is in the Department an Under Secretary for Memorial Affairs, who is appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. The Under Secretary is the head of the National Cemetery Administration as established in and shall perform such functions as may be assigned by the Secretary.section 2400 of this title
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , known as the Department of Veterans Affairs Act, prior to repeal by .section 3(d) of Pub. L. 100–527Pub. L. 102–83, § 3(3)

Amendments1998—, substituted “Under Secretary for Memorial Affairs” for “Director of the National Cemetery System” in section catchline.Pub. L. 105–368, § 403(c)(1)(A), in first sentence, substituted “an Under Secretary for Memorial Affairs” for “a Director of the National Cemetery System” and, in second sentence, substituted “The Under Secretary is the head of the National Cemetery Administration” for “The Director is the head of the National Cemetery System”.Pub. L. 105–368, § 403(a)(3)
